source.sourcePath: raw/sources/DHF (인허가)/03_A1417MCW, A1717MCW, F1417MCW/05_설계개발 검증 및 유효성 확인/15_규격 Test/2. [A1417MCW, A1717MCW, F1417MCW] IEC 60601-1 CB Test report (3.1).pdf

document.title: IEC 60601-1 CB Test Report REP001824 with software, usability, national deviation, user manual, and power supply attachments
document.documentType: CB Scheme test report with attachments
document.revisionOrVersion: Report No. REP001824; usability report REP001824_1; user manual A-TCF-5-130 Version 0.0

sourceSummary.oneLine: This chunk documents passed IEC 62304 software lifecycle clauses, national deviations for Canada/Japan/Korea/USA, IEC 60601-1-6 usability compliance, user manual safety/performance content, and CB certificates for the DEPS9601/DEPS9601A power supplies.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory and CB Scheme submissions by providing safety, usability, software lifecycle, national deviation, marking, power, EMC, and accessory evidence for the A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW digital flat panel X-ray detectors.
keyPoints: IEC 62304 software risk control verification, configuration management, change control, and problem resolution clauses are largely marked Pass with references to Software validation report A-TCF-5-131.
National deviations are included for Canada, Japan, Korea, and the United States under IEC 60601-1.
IEC 60601-1-6 usability report REP001824_1 states the product fulfills IEC 60601-1-6 and EN 60601-1-6 requirements, with process-standard-only evaluation and no testing.

User manual content specifies intended use, contraindications, ratings, Class I/internal power classification, IP54 ingress protection, Type B applied part, EMC precautions, calibration, cleaning, maintenance, and wired/wireless interface details.
Power supply models DEPS9601 and DEPS9601A have IECEE CB Test Certificates for IEC 60601-1:2005 and IEC 60601-1:2005/AMD1:2012.

entities.name: H&abyz Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: IEC 60601-1-6 report lists Applicant’s name as H&abyz Co., Ltd. and Manufacturer as same as applicant.

entities.name: Nemko Korea Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: IEC 60601-1-6 report identifies Nemko Korea Co., Ltd. as the testing laboratory preparing the report and CB Testing Laboratory.

entities.name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
entities.evidence: IEC 60601-1-6 report lists the test item description as Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.

entities.name: A1717MCW; A1417MCW; F1417MCW
entities.evidence: IEC 60601-1-6 report lists Model/Type reference as A1717MCW; A1417MCW; F1417MCW.

entities.name: DAEAN ELECOM CO., LTD.
entities.evidence: CB certificates for DEPS9601 and DEPS9601A list DAEAN ELECOM CO., LTD. as applicant, manufacturer, and factory.

entities.name: DEPS9601
entities.evidence: CB Test Certificate NO82590/A1/M2 identifies product Power supply, model/type ref. DEPS9601.

entities.name: DEPS9601A
entities.evidence: CB Test Certificate NO96755/M1 identifies product Power supply, model/type ref. DEPS9601A.

entities.name: IECEE CB Scheme
entities.evidence: Reports and certificates state test procedure CB Scheme and include IECEE CB Test Certificate wording.

entities.name: KTR
entities.evidence: Republic of Korea National Differences attachment lists Attachment Originator as KTR.

entities.name: UL(US)
entities.evidence: US National Differences attachment lists Attachment Originator as UL(US).

concepts.name: Software risk control verification
concepts.evidence: IEC 62304 clauses 7.3.1 and 7.3.3 are marked Pass with traceability from hazardous situation through software item, cause, risk control, and verification.

concepts.name: Software configuration management and change control
concepts.evidence: IEC 62304 clauses 8.1.3, 8.2.1, 8.2.2, 8.2.3, 8.2.4, and 8.3 are marked Pass with references to Software validation report A-TCF-5-131.

concepts.name: Software problem resolution process
concepts.evidence: IEC 62304 clause 9 requirements for problem reports, investigation, change requests, records, trend analysis, and regression test documentation are marked Pass.

concepts.name: National deviations under IEC 60601-1
concepts.evidence: Attachment 2 lists national deviations for Canada, Japan, Korea, and USA.

concepts.name: IEC 60601-1-6 usability compliance
concepts.evidence: Usability report states the product fulfills IEC 60601-1-6:2010 + AMD1:2013 and EN 60601-1-6:2010 + A1:2015.

concepts.name: Usability engineering file
concepts.evidence: Inspection verified the manufacturer established a usability engineering process, acceptance criteria, and demonstrated acceptance criteria were met.

concepts.name: Digital flat panel X-ray detector intended use
concepts.evidence: Manual states the detector is for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children, not for mammography.

concepts.name: IP54 ingress protection
concepts.evidence: Manual states A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW are rated IP54 for water resistance and dustproofing according to IEC 60529.

concepts.name: EMC compliance and precautions
concepts.evidence: Manual states compliance with IEC 60601-1-2 EMC standards and provides emissions, immunity, and RF separation-distance guidance.

concepts.name: Power supply CB certification
concepts.evidence: CB certificates state DEPS9601 and DEPS9601A samples were tested and found in conformity with IEC 60601-1:2005 and IEC 60601-1:2005/AMD1:2012.

evidence.claim: IEC 62304 software risk control implementation and verification were documented as passing for the evaluated software.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 7.3.1 states implementation of each risk control measure is verified and documented; Software validation report A-TCF-5-131 chapter 11; verdict P.

evidence.claim: Software hazard traceability was documented from hazardous situation through software item, software cause, risk control measure, and verification.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 7.3.3 lists traceability items a-d and references Software validation report A-TCF-5-131 chapter 11; verdict P.

evidence.claim: Software change risk management activities were performed based on analysis of software changes.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 7.4.3 requires risk management activities defined in 7.1, 7.2, and 7.3; Software validation report A-TCF-5-131 chapter 8.3; verdict P.

evidence.claim: The manufacturer documented software system configuration items and versions.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 8.1.3 states configuration items and versions comprising the software system configuration are documented; A-TCF-5-131 chapter 8.3; verdict P.

evidence.claim: Software configuration changes were controlled through approved change requests and verified after implementation.
evidence.evidenceText: Clauses 8.2.1 to 8.2.3 reference A-TCF-5-131 chapters 8.3 and 12 and are marked P for approved change request, implementation, and verification.

evidence.claim: Software problem reporting, investigation, change request handling, records, trend analysis, and regression test documentation were marked compliant.
evidence.evidenceText: Clauses 9.1 through 9.8 are marked P and reference Software validation report A-TCF-5-131 chapter 8.3 for problem resolution activities.

evidence.claim: The software evidence mapping identifies Software validation report A-TCF-5-131 revision 0 dated 2022-09-01 as evidence for many IEC 62304 deliverables.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment Software mapping lists deliverables, title Software validation report (A-TCF-5-131), revision 0, date 2022-09-01.

evidence.claim: Equipment photographs include representative, external, internal, board, power supply, battery, switch box, exposure switch, and interface cable views.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment 1 pages 1-19 list views for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, F1417MCW, main boards, sub board, ROIC board, gate boards, SLM board, DEPS9601/DEPS9601A, rechargeable Li-ion battery, switch box, exposure switch, and interface cable.

evidence.claim: Canadian national differences were evaluated under CAN/CSA C22.2 No. 60601-1:08 and CAN/CSA-C22.2 No. 60601-1:14 including Amendment 1.
evidence.evidenceText: Canadian National Differences attachment states differences according to CAN/CSA C22.2 No. 60601-1:08 and CAN/CSA-C22.2 No. 60601-1:14 including amendment1.

evidence.claim: For Canadian deviations, the power supply box rating was considered acceptable for supply mains voltage.
evidence.evidenceText: Canadian clause 4.10.2 notes the power supply box is rated as 100-240 V~; verdict P.

evidence.claim: For Canadian deviations, the detachable power supply cord detachment was assessed not to constitute safety hazards.
evidence.evidenceText: Canadian clause 8.11.3.2 b)i states accidental detachment does not constitute safety hazards; verdict P.

evidence.claim: Japan national differences were evaluated according to JIS T 0601-1:2017.
evidence.evidenceText: Japan National Differences attachment states differences according to National standard JIS T 0601-1:2017 (IEC 60601-1:2005 + A1:2012(MOD)).

evidence.claim: Japan power supply cord set evaluation was not covered for several clauses.
evidence.evidenceText: Japan clauses 7.7.4, 7.7.5, and 8.11.3.2 state power supply cord set for Japan is not evaluated and refer to the summary of testing; verdict N/A.

evidence.claim: Korea national differences identify national supply voltages of 110, 220, and 380 V, and the product power supply box is rated 100-240 V~.
evidence.evidenceText: Republic of Korea National Differences Voltage row states national supply voltages are 110, 220 V and 380 V; power supply box rated 100-240 V~; verdict P.

evidence.claim: Korea national differences require 60 Hz supply frequency compatibility, and the power supply box rating includes 50/60 Hz.
evidence.evidenceText: Korea Frequency row states only appliances having 60 Hz or a range including 60 Hz are accepted; power supply box rated 50/60 Hz; verdict P.

evidence.claim: Korean safety instructions and markings must be in Korean or acceptable graphical symbols, and the manufacturer was advised of Korean translation requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: Korea Instruction row states manuals and safety markings shall be in Korean or IEC 417 graphical symbols; manufacturer has been advised of Korean translations required; verdict P.

evidence.claim: Korean plug compliance was not evaluated for the power supply cord set.
evidence.evidenceText: Korea Plug row requires KSC 8305 and 8300; power supply cord set for Korea is not evaluated; verdict N/A.

evidence.claim: US national differences were evaluated under AAMI/IEC 60601-1:2005 + AMD1:2012.
evidence.evidenceText: US National Differences attachment states differences according to National standard AAMI/IEC 60601-1:2005 + AMD1:2012.

evidence.claim: US allowable earth leakage current values were evaluated against NFPA 99-derived limits.
evidence.evidenceText: US clause 8.7.3 states earth leakage current allowable values are 5 mA normal condition and 10 mA single fault condition; appended table 8.7; verdict P.

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1-6 usability report REP001824_1 was issued on 2022-11-08 by Nemko Korea Co., Ltd.
evidence.evidenceText: Test report IEC 60601-1-6 lists Report Number REP001824_1, Date of issue 2022-11-08, and testing laboratory Nemko Korea Co., Ltd.

evidence.claim: The usability report covers the A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW digital flat panel X-ray detector models.
evidence.evidenceText: REP001824_1 page 2 lists Test item description Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector and Model/Type reference A1717MCW; A1417MCW; F1417MCW.

evidence.claim: The detector ratings include 3 A at 16 V or internally powered, and the power supply box input/output ratings are specified.
evidence.evidenceText: REP001824_1 page 2 lists detector rating 3 A, 16 V or internally powered; power supply box input 1.0 A, 100-240 V~, 50/60 Hz, Cl. I or 3 A, 24 V; output 3 A, 16 V.

evidence.claim: The IEC 60601-1-6 evaluation was process-standard-only with no physical testing.
evidence.evidenceText: Summary of testing states Tests performed: Process standard only, no testing.

evidence.claim: The product fulfills IEC 60601-1-6:2010 + AMD1:2013 and EN 60601-1-6:2010 + A1:2015.
evidence.evidenceText: Summary of compliance states the product fulfils the requirements of IEC 60601-1-6:2010 + AMD1:2013 and EN 60601-1-6:2010 + A1:2015.

evidence.claim: The device is classified as portable ME equipment in the IEC 60601-1-6 report.
evidence.evidenceText: Test item particulars list Classification of installation and use: Portable ME equipment.

evidence.claim: The usability engineering process, acceptance criteria, and satisfaction of acceptance criteria were verified.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 4.2 states inspection verified the manufacturer established a usability engineering process, established usability acceptance criteria, and demonstrated they were met; verdict P.

evidence.claim: The user manual includes a brief description of equipment, operating principles, and significant physical and performance characteristics relevant to usability.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 60601-1-6 clause 5 states the instructions for use include the required brief description and characteristics; described in the user manual; verdict P.

evidence.claim: The usability engineering file specifies intended indication, patient population, body/tissue interaction, user profile, conditions of use, and operating principle.
evidence.evidenceText: Annex I clause 5.1 references Usability engineering report A-TCF-5-108 chapters 5.1.1 through 5.1.6 for application specification elements; verdict P.

evidence.claim: Known or foreseeable usability hazards and hazardous situations were identified according to ISO 14971.
evidence.evidenceText: Table 5.3.2 states identification of known or foreseeable hazards related to usability according to ISO 14971:2007 Cl. 4.3 was identified; verdict P.

evidence.claim: The usability validation plan specifies validation methods, success criteria, and involvement of representative intended users.
evidence.evidenceText: Table 5.6 states the usability validation plan A-TCF-5-106 specifies validation method, criteria, and representative intended users; verdict P.

evidence.claim: The user manual A-TCF-5-130 is Version 0.0 dated 09/07/2022 and is in English/Korean.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment 4 cover states User manual for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, F1417MCW; A-TCF-5-130 Version: 0.0; 09/07/2022; Language: ENGLISH / KOREAN.

evidence.claim: The user manual indicates the detectors are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors using TFT and CsI scintillator technology.
evidence.evidenceText: Product Features state A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors consisting of TFT and Scintillator CsI.

evidence.claim: The device is indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children and is not for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Indications for use state general radiographic diagnosis targeting adults and children and not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The manual contraindicates use for mammography, direct cardiac application, infants, and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Contraindications state not to be used for mammography and direct cardiac application, and not to be used for infant and pregnant women.

evidence.claim: Patient contact is limited to the front of the detector for less than 10 minutes.
evidence.evidenceText: Applied part and duration of application states patient contacts the front of the detector in less than 10 minutes.

evidence.claim: The device specification lists rated voltage 100-240 V~, 50/60 Hz, maximum 48 W, Class I or internally powered, IP54, and Type B applied part.
evidence.evidenceText: Specification of Device lists rated voltage 100-240 V~, rated frequency 50/60 Hz, electric power consumption MAX 48W, protection type Class I or internally powered, IP54, and Type B applied part for the front panel.

evidence.claim: The detector supports IEEE 802.11 wireless operation and 1 Gbit Ethernet communication.
evidence.evidenceText: Electrical features list Wi-Fi IEEE 802.11 a/b/g/n/ac at 2.4GHz/5GHz and communication 1Giga-bit Ethernet.

evidence.claim: The detector supports Normal Mode and AED Mode.
evidence.evidenceText: Electrical features list Trigger Mode: Normal Mode and AED Mode (Auto Exposure Detection Mode).

evidence.claim: Calibration is required for proper image acquisition and is recommended once every year.
evidence.evidenceText: Calibration section states calibration shall be performed to acquire a proper image and shall be performed once every one year for stabilized detector condition.

evidence.claim: Routine inspection requires daily user checks and yearly vendor recalibration.
evidence.evidenceText: Inspection list includes daily checks for cables, plugs/connectors, cover/parts, LED indicator, and yearly re-calibration by vendor.

evidence.claim: Cleaning instructions specify 70% IPA and prohibit pouring or spraying IPA directly on the detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Cleaning section states clean with 70% IPA, turn off power and remove battery before cleaning, do not pour or spray IPA directly, and wait until IPA is dried completely.

evidence.claim: The EMC declaration states the equipment complies with IEC 60601-1-2 and requires EMC precautions during installation and service.
evidence.evidenceText: EMC Compliance Statement states the equipment complies with IEC 60601-1-2 EMC standards and needs special EMC precautions according to the user manual.

evidence.claim: The EMC emissions declaration classifies RF emissions as CISPR 11 Group 1 and Class A.
evidence.evidenceText: Electromagnetic emissions table lists RF emissions CISPR 11 Group 1 and RF emissions CISPR 11 Class A.

evidence.claim: The wireless interface uses 2.4 GHz and 5 GHz bands with WEP, WPA/WPA2 PSK, and WPA/WPA2 Enterprise security options.
evidence.evidenceText: Wired & Wireless Interface lists wireless frequency 2.4GHz/5GHz dual and security Open, WEP, WPA/WPA2 PSK, WPA/WPA2 Enterprise.

evidence.claim: EU use of 5 GHz Wi-Fi bands 5150-5350 MHz is restricted outdoors.
evidence.evidenceText: Wireless interface note states in the EU, the Wi-Fi function in this equipment should not be used outdoors for 5 GHz bands 5150-5350 MHz.

evidence.claim: The DEPS9601 power supply CB certificate confirms conformity to IEC 60601-1:2005 and IEC 60601-1:2005/AMD1:2012.
evidence.evidenceText: CB Test Certificate NO82590/A1/M2 for DEPS9601 states a sample was tested and found in conformity with IEC 60601-1:2005 and IEC 60601-1:2005/AMD1:2012.

evidence.claim: The DEPS9601A power supply CB certificate confirms conformity to IEC 60601-1:2005 and IEC 60601-1:2005/AMD1:2012.
evidence.evidenceText: CB Test Certificate NO96755/M1 for DEPS9601A states a sample was tested and found in conformity with IEC 60601-1:2005 and IEC 60601-1:2005/AMD1:2012.

evidence.claim: Power supply risk management according to ISO 14971:2007 was not covered in the power supply CB certificates and must be evaluated in the end product.
evidence.evidenceText: Both DEPS9601 and DEPS9601A certificates state risk management process according to ISO 14971:2007 is not covered and must be evaluated in the end product.

evidence.claim: Power supply report 352278 was issued for DEPS9601 and DEPS9601A by Nemko Korea under IEC 60601-1.
evidence.evidenceText: Test Report 352278 lists IEC 60601-1, date of issue 2014-09-18 with Amendment No. 2 on 2018-04-23, models DEPS9601 and DEPS9601A, and Nemko Korea Co., Ltd.

evidence.claim: The power supply was evaluated as Class I, continuous operation, ordinary equipment, not intended for patient connection, and not evaluated for flammable anesthetic mixture use.
evidence.evidenceText: Report 352278 summary clause 6 states Class I, continuous operation, ordinary equipment, not evaluated for flammable anaesthetic mixture use, and not intended for patient connection.

evidence.claim: The DEPS9601A differs from DEPS9601 by model designation and input rating.
evidence.evidenceText: Report 352278 General product information states basic model DEPS9601; variant model DEPS9601A is identical except for model type designation and input rating.

reviewFlags.reason: This is chunk 3 of 6; document-level metadata such as the main IEC 60601-1 report issue date may appear in another chunk.
Attachment 4 user manual text contains OCR/encoding artifacts, although many safety and specification claims remain readable.
Power supply CB certificates explicitly exclude ISO 14971 risk management and require evaluation in the end product.
Several national deviation items are marked N/A because cord sets or ME system requirements for Japan/Korea or other configurations were not evaluated in this report chunk.
